name: Chinese Fortune Cookies #1
recipeInstructions:
1. Have fortunes prepared in advance.
2. Beat egg on low speed until frothy.
3. Gradually beat in sugar, continuing until the mixture is a very light yellow and thick.
4. Mix in corn oil.
5. Blend water with a small amount of the egg mixture into cornstarch, then stir into the remaining egg mix.
6. Heat a heavy, well-seasoned griddle to 350°F (175°C), or until drops of water bounce when placed on the griddle. For griddles without temperature control, keep the heat between low and medium.
7. Drop heaping tablespoons of batter onto the griddle and spread with the back of a spoon to about 4 inches wide and 1/8 inch thick.
8. Cook until the edges are slightly brown and cookies can be easily lifted from the griddle with a spatula, 5-8 minutes. If the cookies stick, cook the bottoms for a bit longer.
9. Carefully turn the cookies over and cook the other side until light brown. Ensure even temperature.
10. Place a fortune paper on the cookie as soon as it is removed from the griddle.
11. Fold the opposite edges together to form a semicircle.
12. Crease crosswise at the center of the straight edge to create a flattened side, then bend the opposing corners together for the traditional shape.
13. Set in a small glass or muffin tin until the cookie cools and holds its shape.
14. Wipe the griddle clean and stir the batter before repeating.
